claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know their age, and if you have multiple children, please tell me the ages of each child you'd like to claim for.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my child Alex, who is 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful! Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 13 years old (under 16), they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Let me continue with the next step of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex working **less than 24 hours per week** in paid employment (or not in paid work at all)?

This is usually straightforward for a 13-year-old, as most children this age aren't in regular paid employment. Please confirm:

- **Yes** - Alex works less than 24 hours per week or doesn't have paid work
- **No** - Alex works 24 hours or more per week

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex doesn't have any paid work at all.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's what I expected for a 13-year-old. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex **NOT receiving** any of the following benefits in their own name?
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

In other words, is Alex free from claiming these benefits themselves?

- **Yes** - Alex is not receiving ESA or Universal Credit in their own right
- **No** - Alex is receiving one of these benefits

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex isn't receiving any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. Let's continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?

- **No** - Alex is not in any apprenticeship
- **Yes** - Alex is in an apprenticeship (please specify which type and country, e.g., England, Scotland, or Wales)

*For your information: Most apprenticeships disqualify a child from Child Benefit, except for Foundation Apprenticeships in Wales which are approved.*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any apprenticeship.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you. Now let's move on to check your eligibility as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check your residency status:**

Do you meet **any one** of the following UK residency requirements?

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You ordinarily reside in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status AND are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status AND are actively looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status AND have enough money to support yourself (and are NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status AND are a student with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

Do any of these apply to you? (You only need to meet **one** of these to qualify)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Just to clarify, there is one situation where you might still be eligible even while living abroad:

**Are you a Crown servant living abroad?**

This would include:
- Diplomatic staff working for the UK government overseas
- Members of the armed forces stationed abroad
- Other government employees posted to foreign countries

Or are you living in certain qualifying countries with specific agreements with the UK?

- **Yes** - I am a Crown servant or in a qualifying country
- **No** - I am living abroad for other reasons (not as a Crown servant)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant. I'm just living abroad for personal reasons.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. Unfortunately, based on the information you've provided, I need to navigate to the outcome for residency requirements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry, but based on your answers, I've reached a decision on your Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Assessment Outcome: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

I'm sorry to inform you that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| Alex | 13 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet UK residency requirements |

### Explanation

While Alex meets all the requirements for a qualifying child (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), **you** as the claimant do not meet the UK residency requirements because you are living abroad for personal reasons.

### What are the residency requirements?

To claim Child Benefit, you must:
- **Normally live in the UK**, OR
- Have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme, OR
- Have **pre-settled status** with qualifying conditions (working, seeking work, studying, or have sufficient resources), OR
- Be a **Crown servant** living abroad

### What can you do?

1. **If your circumstances change** - If you move back to the UK or become normally resident here, you may then be eligible to claim Child Benefit.

2. **Check for other support** - Depending on the country where you're living, there may be equivalent child-related benefits available locally.

3. **Get further advice** - If you believe your situation is more complex or you have questions, you can contact HMRC directly or visit [GOV.UK Child Benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility) for more information.

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex (aged 13) meets all the child-related eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in their own name, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad for personal reasons and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and does not normally live in the UK. Therefore, the claim for Alex is not eligible.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK, have settled or pre-settled status (with qualifying conditions), or be a Crown servant living abroad. Since none of these apply, the claim cannot proceed.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad for personal reasons and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and does not normally live in the UK. Therefore, the claim for Alex is not eligible.
